description: Phase 7: Agentic AI Foundation. Extend @quant/agent-runtime with a typed tool registry, permission-scoped agent execution engine, plan generator with approval workflow, cost tracking, safety classification, and implement 5+ end-to-end agent workflows. Every agent action must have permission, approval, audit, and undo story.
steps: 1. In packages/agent-runtime/src/types.ts (create new file), define comprehensive types: AgentActionTier (Tier0_ReadOnly, Tier1_DraftOnly, Tier2_LowRisk, Tier3_HighRisk, Tier4_Admin), ToolDefinition (name, description, parameters with JSON Schema, requiredTier, handler), AgentPlan (id, steps with tier, status, approvals needed), AgentWorkflowResult (success, actions taken, undoable actions, audit entries, cost), CostEstimate (model, tokens, estimatedCost), SafetyClassification (safe/caution/blocked with reason).

2. In packages/agent-runtime/src/tool-registry.ts (new file), implement TypedToolRegistry class: registerTool(tool: ToolDefinition), getTool(name), getToolsByTier(tier), getAllTools(), validateArgs(toolName, args) using zod. This is distinct from the AI package ToolRegistry - it is the runtime-level registry with permission tiers.

3. In packages/agent-runtime/src/plan-generator.ts (new file), implement PlanGenerator class: generatePlan(intent, context, availableTools) -> AgentPlan. Plans are multi-step, each step has a tool, args, tier, and approval requirement. Steps are editable by user before execution. Include estimateCost(plan) method.

4. In packages/agent-runtime/src/safety-classifier.ts (new file), implement SafetyClassifier class: classify(action, context) -> SafetyClassification. Rules: PII access without consent = blocked, financial actions above threshold = caution, admin actions = caution, content moderation override = blocked. Include configurable rule engine.

5. In packages/agent-runtime/src/cost-tracker.ts (new file), implement CostTracker class: recordCost(agentId, workflowId, cost), getTotalCost(agentId, period), getWorkflowCost(workflowId), isWithinBudget(agentId, estimatedCost), setBudget(agentId, budget, period). Track per-agent and per-workflow costs with budget enforcement.

6. In packages/agent-runtime/src/execution-engine.ts (new file), implement ExecutionEngine class that ties everything together: executePlan(plan, context) method that for each step: (a) checks permission via PermissionGuard, (b) runs safety classifier, (c) if tier >= 2 submits to ApprovalQueue and waits, (d) executes tool, (e) logs to AuditTrail, (f) registers undo action in UndoEngine, (g) tracks cost. Include human handoff for blocked/failed steps.

7. In packages/agent-runtime/src/workflows/ (new directory), implement 5 end-to-end agent workflows as classes extending a BaseWorkflow abstract class: (a) PlanMyDayWorkflow - reads calendar, email, tasks, produces daily plan (Tier 0), (b) EmailReplyWorkflow - drafts replies to emails in user style (Tier 1), (c) MeetingToTasksWorkflow - converts meeting notes to tasks and doc (Tier 2 with confirmation), (d) CrossAppSearchWorkflow - finds files/docs/emails related to a topic (Tier 0), (e) ContentLaunchWorkflow - creates post, video caption, email, and ad campaign (Tier 3 with explicit approval). Each workflow defines its tools, plan, and undo strategy.

8. Create packages/agent-runtime/src/workflows/base-workflow.ts with BaseWorkflow abstract class: abstract getTools(), abstract buildPlan(input), execute(input) that uses ExecutionEngine, getAuditTrail(), getUndoableActions(), getCost().
9. Update packages/agent-runtime/src/index.ts to export all new modules: TypedToolRegistry, PlanGenerator, SafetyClassifier, CostTracker, ExecutionEngine, BaseWorkflow, and all 5 workflow classes.

10. Write comprehensive tests in packages/agent-runtime/src/__tests__/: tool-registry.test.ts (register, lookup, validate), plan-generator.test.ts (generate plan, cost estimate), safety-classifier.test.ts (classify actions, rule engine), cost-tracker.test.ts (record, budget, period), execution-engine.test.ts (full pipeline: permission check -> safety -> approval -> execute -> audit -> undo -> cost), workflows.test.ts (each of 5 workflows executes end-to-end with mocked tools, verifying permission/approval/audit/undo/cost story).
11. Run pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm build, pnpm lint and fix any failures.
acceptance_criteria: TypedToolRegistry exists with tier-based tool registration and zod validation
PlanGenerator creates multi-step plans with cost estimates
SafetyClassifier blocks unsafe actions (PII without consent, unauthorized admin)
ExecutionEngine implements full pipeline: permission -> safety -> approval -> execute -> audit -> undo -> cost
5 end-to-end workflows exist (PlanMyDay, EmailReply, MeetingToTasks, CrossAppSearch, ContentLaunch)
Every workflow has permission, approval, audit, and undo story (verified by tests)
All action tiers (0-4) are represented across the 5 workflows
pnpm test passes with new tests
verification: pnpm test (must pass all suites including new agent-runtime tests)
findings: All 312 tests pass (40 test files). Typecheck passes across all 72 packages. Build and lint pass. Prettier applied via lint-staged on commit. The CostTracker getSpendingHistory sort depends on timestamp resolution - records inserted at the same millisecond may not sort deterministically.
